chapter_title.ar: باب فِيمَا يَأْخُذُ السُّلْطَانُ مِنَ الْخَرَاجِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 25 – Regarding what the Sultan (ruling authority) takes from the taxes

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/79)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/79)

parts.text: أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِي نَصْرٍ

text: سَأَلْتُهُ عَنِ الرَّجُلِ يَرِثُ الْأَرْضَ أَوْ يَشْتَرِيهَا فَيُؤَدِّي خَرَاجَهَا إِلَى السُّلْطَانِ هَلْ عَلَيْهِ عُشْرٌ قَالَ لَا .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Abu Nasr, from Rifa’at Bin Musa,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullahasws, said, ‘I asked himasws about the man who inherits a land, or he buys it, and he pays its taxes to the Sultan (ruling authority), ‘Is there a tenth (10%) to pay upon it (as Zakāt)?’ Heasws said: ‘No’.
en.sarwar: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from ibn abu Nasr from Rifa‘ah ibn Musa who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who inherits a piece of land or buys it then pays its taxes to al-Sultan (the ruler), is he required to pay one out of ten (as Zakat)? He (the Imam) said, ‘No, he is not required to pay.’”
